title: Pain perceived during hysteroscopic morcellation by vaginoscopy vs standard technique in an outpatient setting: a randomized controlled trial
rights_license_short: Creative Commons Zero v1.0 Universal cc0-1.0 https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/legalcode | info:eu-repo/semantics/openAccess
related_publications_short: Dataverse publication:1. Fritz MA, Speroff L. Clinical gynecologic endocrinology and infertility. 8the éd. Philadelphia: Wolters Kluwer Health/Lippincott Williams & Wilkins; 2011. 2. American Association of Gynecologic L. AAGL practice report: practice guidelines for the diagnosis and management of endometrial polyps. J Minim Invasive Gynecol. 2012;19(1):3-10. Epub 2011/12/27. 3. Friedman JA, Wong JMK, Chaudhari A, Tsai S, Milad MP. Hysteroscopic myomectomy: a comparison of techniques and rev...
description_short: Design: A randomized controlled trial Setting: Outpatient fertility clinic a tertiary university hospital Population: Women between the ages 18 to 52 years; with a clear indication for operative hysteroscopy such as a polyp or type 0/1 fibroid measuring < 2cm, or < 4 cm total when more than 1 lesion was present. Interventions: Patients were randomized into 2 groups: group 1 underwent hysteroscopic morcellation by standard technique (paracervical block and cervical dilation), group 2 underwent hysteroscopic morcellation by vaginoscopy. The primary outcome measured was the participant’s self-rated pain perception using the 10-point visual analog scale after the procedure. The secondary outcomes included duration of the intervention, blood loss, any other reported complication and time to pregnancy.

description_short: This dataset contains the replication code and anonymized data for the paper 'Spillover Effect of One-off Subsidies on Long-Run Health Products Adoption: Experimental Evidence from Free Eyeglasses in Rural China'. Below is the abstract of the paper:One-off subsidies are widely used to promote health product adoption, yet existing evidence largely overlooks spillover effects on non-recipients—particularly in settings with strong social interactions between recipients and their non-recipients peers, such as among adolescents. Using a randomized controlled trial conducted in rural junior high schools in China, we study the effects of providing free, high-quality eyeglasses to myopic students on subsequent eyeglass adoption among non-recipient classmates who later develop vision impairment. description_short: This repository includes the dataset, Stata do files, and survey codebook to reproduce the analysis of the manuscript "The impact of sustainability-focused nudges on the healthfulness of food selections: A randomized trial of young adults." This was an online randomized controlled trial with a convenience sample of 2,149 US young adults ages 18-25 years old. Participants were randomized to the experimental (n=1,074) or control (n=1,075) arm and were instructed to select one food they would most like to purchase in three different food categories: proteins, frozen meals, and snacks (17 foods/category). Foods in the experimental arm included front-of-package labels that communicated the climate impact of the food (low/medium/high) based on the food’s carbon footprint (operationalized as the food’s greenhouse gas emissions (kg CO2-eq/100g)). description_short: The archived data are from the follow-up study of a previously completed IES-funded Goal 3 RCT (PI: Tolan) that tested the impact of combined training in the PAX Good Behavior Game, which focuses on group-based classroom behavior management that systematically emphasizes empirically-based practices in organization of the classroom, and My Teaching Partner, a coaching support model that focuses on promoting positive teacher-student interactions. The integrated program, referred to as “GBG+MTP”, focused on early-career elementary school teachers (first 3 years of teaching in grades K-3), with the goal of improving teacher engagement in and skill with managing the classroom, which in turn would lead to improvements in the classroom learning environment and student behavior and achievement. title: Uncomplicated malaria features and efficacy of ASAQ in DR Congo.dta
description_short: Data related to the manuscript entitled:Uncomplicated clinical malaria features, the efficacy of artesunate-amodiaquine and their relation with multiplicity of infection in the Democratic Republic of Congo. This was a cohort that allowed to describe uncomplicated malaria features and assess efficacy of artesunate-amodiaquine. Participants were followed up during 42 days. Clinical failures had to be recruited in a randomized clinical trial to assess the efficacy and safety of rescue treatments.
